examples: A pitcher or a flytrap cannot carry out much photosynthesis because, unlike plants with ordinary leaves, they do not have flat solar panels that can grab lots of sunlight.
The leaves of the Venus flytrap plant are covered in hairs.
Venus flytrap snaps shut if its tiny hairs are brushed twice.
In her reduced circumstances, the walk to Sixty-ninth Street was a Venus flytrap flushed with colour and shining with adhesive dew.
